data.title: Stock Today: CIMB Eases 0.64% As Investors Weigh Stable Earnings Outlook
data.description:
CIMB Group Holdings Bhd shares eased, slipping 0.64% or 0.05 to RM7.75 as of 11.31 am, after opening at RM7.83 and moving within a range of RM7.72 to RM7.84 with volume recorded at 20,435 lots.

The softer share performance came even as recent research coverage remained broadly supportive, with RHB Investment Bank Bhd, MBSB Investment Bank Bhd and Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd all maintaining BUY calls on CIMB Group Holdings Bhd.

Target prices ranged between RM9.00 and RM9.80, with analysts pointing to resilient earnings visibility, strong non-interest income momentum and sustained dividend yields above 6% as key support factors despite ongoing macro and geopolitical uncertainties.

Market sentiment towards CIMB was underpinned by expectations that the group will deliver stable 1Q26 results, with analysts highlighting that non-interest income is likely to be a key driver through trading, foreign exchange, wealth management, bancassurance and structured products.

However, they noted that overall earnings strength is expected to remain broadly flat year-on-year with moderate sequential improvement, reflecting a mixed operating environment across regional markets.

Analysts also said CIMB’s direct exposure to Middle East tensions remains immaterial, with no visible deterioration in asset quality so far.

However, they flagged that indirect risks linked to inflationary pressures, demand moderation and sectoral stress remain part of ongoing monitoring, with stress testing frameworks being intensified across geographies and customer segments.

On capital and income trends, CIMB is expected to maintain stable net interest margins supported by disciplined deposit pricing, while liquidity conditions in Malaysia and Singapore remain comfortable. Credit costs are forecast to stay within guidance of 25 to 35 basis points, reinforcing expectations of stable earnings delivery.

Despite short-term share price weakness, analysts continue to view CIMB as supported by strong capital buffers, attractive dividend policy and improving non-interest income mix, which they said underpin its defensive earnings profile within the banking sector.

data.title: KLCI Dips At Midday As Heavyweights Weigh On Sentiment Amid Broad Regional Weakness
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended its softer tone at midday on April 22, with the benchmark index drifting lower as persistent selling in index heavyweights tracked weaker regional equity performance.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 6.32 points, or 0.37%, to 1,709.01, as investors remained cautious following its close of 1,715.33 on the previous session.

The broader market also stayed under mild pressure, with most indices in the red, including the FBM 70, which eased 8.34 points (0.37%) to 17,842.70 and FBM EMAS declining 6.44 points (0.05%) to 12,465.69, reflecting a risk-off mood across the board.

Market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 560 to 448, while 540 counters were unchanged. A further 1,211 counters went untraded, underscoring subdued participation, alongside 35 suspended counters.

Total turnover reached 1.88 billion units, with value traded at RM1.34 billion, indicating selective trading activity despite overall weakness.

Blue-chip selling pressure remained the key drag on sentiment, with losses concentrated in financial and energy-linked counters.

Among the top losers, Malayan Banking Bhd and Public Bank Bhd peers in the financial space weighed on the index, alongside weakness in energy-related names such as Petronas Gas Bhd, which declined amid broader sector consolidation.

Trading activity remained elevated in select penny and speculative counters. Zetrix AI Bhd led volumes, while NexG Bhd and Perdana Petroleum Bhd saw active two-way flows, reflecting continued retail interest in lower-priced equities.

AirAsia X operator AirAsia X Bhd was among the more actively traded laggards, while VS Industry Bhd traded flat amid mixed sentiment in manufacturing-linked names.

Upside was led by defensive and consumer-linked counters, with Petronas Dagangan Bhd among the top gainers, alongside select plantation and consumer staples names.

Financial heavyweight insurers also saw mild gains, with Allianz Malaysia Bhd edging higher on steady defensive demand.

Overall, the midday session reflected a market lacking strong catalysts, with investors staying defensive amid global uncertainty and mixed regional cues.

Analysts expect near-term trading on Bursa Malaysia to remain range-bound, with index direction largely dictated by heavyweight banking and energy stocks in the absence of fresh domestic catalysts.

data.title: Stock Today: Bumi Armada Gains 1.35% Amid Digital Expertise-Driven Board Addition
data.description:
Bumi Armada Bhd shares traded high at RM0.375 as at 2.53 pm, up 0.005 or 1.35% from the previous close, with intraday trading ranging between RM0.370 and RM0.380 on a volume of 62,276 lots.

The counter opened at RM0.375 and moved in a tight range throughout the session, reflecting steady investor interest despite relatively muted volatility. Buying support was seen at RM0.375 with sell orders capped at RM0.380, suggesting a balanced order flow during the trading day.

The latest price movement came against a backdrop of recent corporate developments, including the appointment of Google Cloud Malaysia Country Manager Puan Raja Farhana Raja Nong Chik Najmuddin as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective April 21, 2026.

The company said the appointment is aimed at strengthening board expertise in digital transformation and technology-driven strategy as it navigates evolving trends in the offshore energy sector.

Her appointment is seen as aligning with Bumi Armada’s broader push towards integrating digital capabilities within its operational and strategic framework.

Market sentiment towards the stock remains influenced by energy sector developments as well as broader expectations on offshore activity recovery, with investors continuing to assess the group’s positioning in a transitioning global energy landscape.

Despite the modest gain, trading activity remained relatively contained, indicating cautious accumulation as market participants weigh both sector dynamics and recent governance-related developments within the company.

data.title: Stock Today: Gamuda Gains Slightly Amid RM1.7 Billion Data Centre Win
data.description:
Gamuda Bhd’s shares exhibited firmer trading activity in the afternoon, increasing by 0.22% to RM4.46 as of 4.36 pm.

Analysts from RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research), MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research), and Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) have all sustained BUY recommendations for the stock, following its RM1.7 billion hyperscale data center contract win in Port Dickson. This is attributed to factors such as orderbook expansion, enhanced earnings visibility, and the strengthening of data center exposure.

The stock opened at RM4.43, moved between RM4.39 and RM4.47 during the session, and saw active trading volume of 153.59 million shares. The latest close compares with a previous indicative level of RM5.14 recorded in December 2025.

The contract from a US-headquartered multinational corporation involves the development of a single-storey hyperscale data centre covering site infrastructure works, core and shell construction and mechanical, electrical and plumbing fit-out services. Works are scheduled to begin in the second quarter of CY26 and are expected to be completed in the first quarter of CY28.

RHB Research said it maintained BUY with a revised target price of RM6.01, down from RM6.26, noting that the win reinforces Gamuda’s construction pipeline strength amid rising global demand for data centre capacity.

MBSB Research kept its BUY call with an unchanged target price of RM5.60, stating that the contract is broadly in line with expectations and keeps Gamuda on track to meet its RM50 billion to RM55 billion orderbook target by end-CY26.

HLIB also reiterated BUY, revising its target price to RM5.31 from RM5.60, while noting that despite a slower start to CY26, further replenishment from infrastructure, water and renewable energy projects alongside additional data centre jobs should support growth.

Analysts highlighted that the data centre segment now makes up about 11% of Gamuda’s RM45.5 billion orderbook, with continued tender activity across Australia, Taiwan and Malaysia expected to act as further catalysts for earnings momentum.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Almost Flat Against Dollar Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Extension
data.description:
The ringgit opened almost flat against the US dollar on Wednesday as market sentiment remained cautious following news that the United States-Iran ceasefire, originally expected to expire today, has been extended to allow negotiations with Iran to conclude, according to Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency was nearly unchanged at 3.9495/9570 versus the greenback compared with 3.9490/9530 at yesterday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said US President Donald Trump, in a last minute bid, announced that the ceasefire would continue until both sides reach an agreement although there was no confirmation in this matter by Iranian officials.

“It appears the market would need to familiarise itself with the inconsistent messaging. While it does create a high degree of uncertainties, it also suggests some indecisiveness, leading to hopes that a compromise could be achieved at some point,” he told Bernama.

Mohd Afzanizam said West Texas Intermediate (WTI) and Brent crude prices continued to climb by 2.57 per cent and 3.74 per cent to US$89.67 per barrel and US$99.05 per barrel as the prospects for a truce between the US and Iran looked dim. 

“Meanwhile, retail sales in the US grew more than expected in March, rising 0.7 per cent month-on-month against consensus estimate of 0.2 per cent.

“However, excluding gas and autos, retail sales sustained at 0.6 per cent, suggesting that higher gasoline prices in March was the main reason for higher retail sales. This could mean Americans’ purchasing power is reducing as they need to allocate more budget for fuel at the expense of other products,” he said.

In another development, Mohd Afzanizam said Federal Reserve chairman nominee Kevin Warsh remained consistent in his stance on central bank independence during the Senate Banking Commitee hearing on Tuesday, promising for a new framework in dealing with higher cost of living issues although no details were spelled out.

He said the US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.32 per cent to 98.414 points.

“On that note, the ringgit will remain in a narrow range of between RM3.94 to RM3.96 today as traders and investors will be observing the eventual outcome in the war in Iran,” Mohd Afzanizam added. 

At the opening, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.

It weakened versus the British pound to 5.3366/3467 from 5.3323/3377 at yesterday’s close, but edged up against the euro to 4.6391/6479 from 4.6436/6483 previously and gained vis-a-vis the Japanese yen to 2.4785/4834 from 2.4802/4829.

The local currency traded mixed against its ASEAN peers.

It rose versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1018/1079 from yesterday’s close of 3.1043/1077, appreciated against the Thai baht to 12.2709/3022 from 12.2983/3162 on Tuesday, but it was flat vis-a-vis the Indonesian rupiah at 230.3/230.9 versus 230.3/230.7 yesterday and stood firm at 6.59/6.60 against the Philippine peso.

data.title: KLCI Snaps 4-Day Rally As Profit-Taking Hits
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on April 22, snapping a four-day winning streak as investors locked in gains amid mixed regional market cues and softer trading momentum.

At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 4.94 points, or 0.29%, to close at 1,710.39, retreating from its previous level of 1,715.33.

The broader market also eased, with FBM 70 and FBM EMAS indices edging lower at 17,835.27 and 12,618.02, respectively, after declining 15.77 points (0.09%) and 30.06 points (0.24%), reflecting a cautious tone across mid- and large-cap counters. However, the FBM SHA bucked the trend with a marginal gain of 9.06 points (0.07%) to 12,481.19.

Market participants attributed the pullback to profit-taking after recent gains, compounded by uneven performance across regional equity markets.

Turnover also moderated, with trading volume easing to 3.19 billion units worth RM2.69 billion, down from 3.45 billion units valued at RM3.18 billion in the previous session.

Activity remained concentrated in speculative and lower-priced stocks. Zetrix AI Bhd led the most active list, followed by airline operator AirAsia X Bhd and tech-related counter NexG Bhd, which saw mixed price movements amid high retail participation.

Oil and gas-related penny stock Borneo Oil Bhd remained heavily traded, while property-linked Perdana Petroleum Bhd saw selective buying interest.

Despite the pullback, market breadth remained relatively stable, suggesting underlying resilience as rotational buying persisted in select sectors.

Analysts said the market’s near-term direction is likely to remain range-bound, with investors taking cues from global risk sentiment and continued profit-taking after the recent rally.

For now, Bursa Malaysia appears to be entering a consolidation phase, with heavyweight stocks expected to continue dictating index direction in the absence of fresh domestic catalysts.

data.title: Wall Street Slips As Middle East Tensions Outweigh Earnings Optimism
data.description:
US stocks ended lower on Tuesday after early gains faded, as renewed concerns over conflict in the Middle East overshadowed upbeat corporate earnings and strong economic data.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 293.18 points or 0.59%, while the S&P 500 declined 0.63% and the Nasdaq Composite slipped 0.59%.

Markets initially moved higher on optimism around earnings and artificial intelligence-driven growth but reversed course in late trading following reports that diplomatic momentum involving Iran and the United States had weakened.

Investors were also reacting to heightened uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict, which has emerged as a key market driver in recent sessions.

“There’s two things going on – what is the resolution going to be or the path going to be for Iran, but in the meantime if that wasn’t there, you’ve got really good expectations for earnings coming in and the companies are pretty much reporting that way, and the economy is doing fine,” said Thomas Martin, senior portfolio manager at GLOBALT Investments.

“The wild card is indeed what happens with Iran, and nobody knows, and it’s baffling to me to think that people think that it’s going to be OK,” he added.

Economic data showed US retail sales rose 1.7% in March, the strongest increase in a year, driven partly by higher gasoline prices and increased service station receipts.

Despite broader weakness in equities, the S&P 500 energy index was the only major sector to post gains, rising 1.31% as oil prices climbed on geopolitical concerns.

Among individual movers, UnitedHealth Group jumped 7% after raising its full-year profit forecast and beating first-quarter expectations, making it the biggest positive contributor to the Dow.

In contrast, Apple fell 2.52% after announcing leadership changes, including the planned succession of CEO Tim Cook by long-time hardware executive John Ternus.

Sentiment was further shaped by comments from US Federal Reserve nominee Kevin Warsh, who said he had made no commitments to President Donald Trump on interest rate cuts and stressed the importance of central bank independence.

Market breadth remained negative, with declining stocks outnumbering advancers more than two to one on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq.

data.title: HSIF Slips Below 26,600 Resistance, Short Bias Kept
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to hold short positions after the index retreated sharply from key resistance levels.

The research house said HSIF fell 331 points to close at 26,161 points, after opening at 26,487 points and sliding to an intraday low of 26,085 points before the close. In after-hours trading, the index edged up slightly by 8 points to 26,169 points.

RHB Research noted that the rejection at the 26,600-point level signals a return of bearish momentum, suggesting sellers have regained control of the market. It added that the latest pullback indicates potential price weakness, supported by a declining Relative Strength Index.

The firm expects the index to trade within a range in the near term before testing immediate support at 25,100 points. A further downside could see HSIF moving towards the next support level at 24,500 points.

Traders were advised to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risk.

On the upside, RHB Research identified 26,600 points as immediate resistance, followed by a higher level at 27,200 points, as the market continues to navigate near-term volatility.

data.title: Gainers Edge Ahead On Bursa As Select Stocks Shine Amid Mixed Trading
data.description:
Gainers held a slight edge at Bursa Malaysia’s close, with buying interest in select heavyweights offsetting broader caution as market sentiment remained mixed.

Among the top performers, Malaysian Pacific Industries led the gainers’ list, rising RM2.40 to RM36.60 on active interest. Financial counters also featured prominently, with Hong Leong Bank advancing 56 sen to RM22.96, while Hong Leong Financial Group gained 46 sen to RM19.34, reflecting steady support in the banking sector.

Consumer and industrial-linked stocks also saw selective strength, with Dutch Lady Milk edging up 36 sen to RM32.38, albeit on thin trading volume. Meanwhile, UWC climbed 23 sen to RM4.78, supported by comparatively heavier turnover.

On the downside, selling pressure was evident in several plantation and consumer-linked counters. Kuala Lumpur Kepong slipped 28 sen to RM20.60, while Fraser & Neave Holdings declined 26 sen to RM31.20 amid mild profit-taking.

Other laggards included Bintulu Port Holdings, which fell 24 sen to RM5.35 on light trading, and Petronas Dagangan Bhd, which eased 20 sen to RM20.00. Petronas Chemicals Group also came under pressure, losing 19 sen to RM5.12 despite relatively active turnover.

Overall, the session reflected a stock-specific market, with gains concentrated in selected financial and industrial names while broader sentiment remained cautious amid ongoing rotation and profit-taking in defensive and commodity-linked sectors.

data.title: Market Movement Ends Mixed As Losers Edge Ahead
data.description:
The stock market movement closed on a cautious note, with decliners narrowly outpacing gainers as weakness in selected heavyweights offset pockets of late-session buying interest.

Market breadth remained negative, with 584 losers versus 565 gainers, while 596 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,014 counters were untraded and 35 suspended, reflecting a largely subdued trading environment.

Losses among key blue chips dragged on overall market performance, led by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, which fell sharply, alongside financial heavyweight Hong Leong Bank Bhd and conglomerate Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd.

Consumer names were also under pressure, with Heineken Malaysia Bhd and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd retreating amid mild profit-taking.

On the upside, defensive and consumer staples counters helped cushion the broader market. Nestlé (M) Bhd led gainers, followed by Petronas Dagangan Bhd, reflecting continued demand for resilient earnings plays.

Industrial and technology-linked counters also saw selective interest, with UWC Bhd among the most actively traded gainers, while Hong Leong Industries Bhd and plantation player Batu Kawan Bhd posted modest gains.

Despite the near-balanced market breadth, the overall tone remained cautious, with investors rotating into defensives while trimming positions in cyclical and high-beta names.

Analysts said the market is likely to stay range-bound in the near term, with sentiment hinging on external cues and the performance of heavyweight stocks, which continue to dictate overall direction.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher, Tracking Regional Rebound Despite Global Jitters
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened firmer, with key indices advancing in early trade as local sentiment tracked a mixed but resilient regional tone despite lingering geopolitical uncertainty and a weaker US overnight performance.

The FBM KLCI rose 3.74 points or 0.22% to 1,719.07 at the open, after trading between 1,713.47 and 1,720.46.

Broader market indices also moved higher. The FBM70 gained 37.02 points or 0.21% to 17,888.06, while the FBM EMAS climbed 33.25 points or 0.26% to 12,681.33. The FBM Small Cap index advanced 43.65 points or 0.35% to 12,515.78, and the FBM Emas Shariah added 2.38 points or 0.23% to 1,023.68.

Market breadth was supported by active early trading, with selected small-cap counters driving volume.

Among the most actively traded stocks, NexG led with 297.88 million shares, up 0.02 sen to 32.5 sen. Perdana gained 1 sen to 17.5 sen with 245.36 million shares, while Zetrix rose 1.5 sen to 91 sen on 226.29 million shares.

VS added 1 sen to 21.5 sen, while DNEX advanced 1 sen to 34 sen, both on elevated volumes.

Blue-chip counters led top gainers. Nestle rose 46 sen to RM98.48, PetDag added 40 sen to RM20.40, and KLK gained 24 sen to RM20.84. PChem climbed 17 sen to RM5.29, while HLIND advanced 24 sen to RM17.60.

On the downside, MPI slipped 54 sen to RM36.06, while HEIM, Time dotCom, Kotra and JCBNext also posted modest losses.

The firmer opening in Kuala Lumpur came despite a softer Wall Street session overnight, where US equities closed lower as renewed concerns over the Middle East conflict overshadowed earlier optimism from earnings and strong economic data.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.59%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq both declined 0.63% and 0.59% respectively.

Sentiment globally was also shaped by continued uncertainty over US-Iran tensions, which have been a key driver of volatility in recent sessions, alongside mixed corporate earnings and shifting expectations on interest rates.

data.title: Wall Street Hits Records As Ceasefire Hopes Lift Sentiment
data.description:
US stocks climbed to fresh highs on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing at record levels, as investors responded positively to an extension of the Iran ceasefire alongside a strong start to the earnings season.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 340.65 points or 0.69% to 49,490.03, while the S&P 500 gained 73.89 points or 1.05% to 7,137.90. The Nasdaq Composite outperformed, advancing 397.60 points or 1.64% to end at 24,657.57.

The rally came after US President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely following mediation efforts by Pakistan, although tensions remain unresolved as the US naval blockade of Iranian ports continues and disruptions persist in the Strait of Hormuz.

The waterway, which accounts for about 20% of global oil supply, remains a key uncertainty for markets. Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said a full ceasefire would only be meaningful if the blockade is lifted.

Markets have been buoyed in recent weeks by expectations of a possible de-escalation, even as risks tied to energy supply and inflation linger with oil prices hovering near US$100 per barrel.

“Everyone’s kind of sick of it… clearly, the market is looking for a beneficial outcome or some kind of decent outcome here,” said Stephen Massocca, senior vice president at Wedbush Securities in San Francisco.

“Earnings have been good – now, will they continue to be good if we continue to be at war – it’s going to lose a little bit of its oomph. That said, in my world, there’s still tremendous value, there’s a lot of really cheap stuff out there.”

Technology stocks led gains, with the S&P 500 tech index rising 2.31%, supported by chipmakers. Micron Technology surged 8.48% to a record close, while the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index extended its rally to a sixteenth straight session.

Corporate earnings continued to underpin sentiment, with first quarter growth tracking around 14%, according to LSEG data. GE Vernova jumped 13.75% after raising its annual revenue forecast, while Boston Scientific gained 8.99% on strong quarterly results. Boeing climbed 5.53% after posting a smaller-than-expected loss.

However, not all stocks advanced. United Airlines fell 5.58% after guiding lower profit expectations due to rising fuel costs.

Market breadth remained positive, with advancing stocks outnumbering decliners on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq, as investors continued to balance optimism over earnings with lingering geopolitical risks.

data.title: Stock Picks: Sime Darby Property And QES Group
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Sime Darby Property Bhd and QES Group Bhd as stocks with near-term upside potential, citing bullish technical breakouts supported by strong momentum.

The research house said Sime Darby Property is poised to extend its gains after breaking above the RM1.35 resistance level with strong trading volume. It noted that the stock formed a bullish Marubozu candlestick, signalling firm buying interest and positive momentum.

RHB Research expects the counter to test the next resistance levels at RM1.43 and RM1.51 if the upward trend continues. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM1.27 support level could weaken sentiment and trigger a reversal.

Meanwhile, QES Group has also entered a bullish phase after closing above the RM0.42 resistance level. The stock registered a “higher high” candlestick pattern, indicating strengthening upward momentum.

The research house said QES is likely to target the next resistance levels at RM0.45 and RM0.48, supported by sustained buying interest. On the downside, it flagged RM0.39 as a key support level, with a breach potentially leading to renewed selling pressure.

RHB Research noted that both counters are currently riding on positive technical setups, with momentum-driven trading expected to guide near-term price movements.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 21, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 21, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: US Stocks Rallied On Tim Cook’s Exit And Kevin Warsh Appointment
data.description:
US stocks rallied on Tuesday as investors weighed Tim Cook’s exit as Apple CEO and looked ahead to the confirmation hearing for Fed Chair appointee Kevin Warsh

The Dow Jones Industrial Average led gains, up 0.8%. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 rose 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.2% on the heels of mild closing losses for Wall Street stocks.Kevin Warsh’s Senate confirmation hearing began at 10 a.m. ET. In prepared remarks, the Federal Reserve chair nominee indicated that the push-and-pull between Trump and the central bank may be coming to an end. Largely absent were any firm statements on the outlook for interest rates.

Meanwhile, Wall Street is digesting the changing of the guard at Apple, which will see longtime CEO Tim Cook step down and hand over the CEO role to hardware engineering head John Ternus. The iPhone maker’s stock slipped 0.7% in the first minutes of Tuesday trading after the late Monday news.

On the economic data, US retail sales rose by the most in a year, notching a 1.7% monthly rise, according to Commerce Department data released Tuesday. The hotter-than-expected read is a bullish signal that Americans are continuing to spend even as gasoline prices have cut into budgets.

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Stock Picks: Hong Leong Financial And Ranhill
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd and Ranhill Utilities Bhd as stocks to watch, citing bullish technical setups following recent breakouts.

RHB Research said Hong Leong Financial’s upward movement may resume after the counter broke above the RM19.20 resistance level, supported by a bullish candlestick formation.

The stock has also moved above its 21-day simple moving average, indicating a strengthening near-term trend. Riding on the momentum, the counter could advance towards RM20.20, with further upside at RM20.90.

However, the research house noted that a fall below the RM18.50 support level would negate the bullish setup.

Meanwhile, Ranhill Utilities is expected to extend its gains after breaking past the RM1.86 resistance on strong trading volume.

The counter formed a long bullish candlestick, signalling renewed buying interest. RHB Research said this momentum could drive the stock towards RM1.97, followed by RM2.08.

On the downside, a breach below the RM1.75 support level would invalidate the bullish view and signal a potential correction.

data.title: RHB Keeps Negative Bias On HSIF Despite Strengthening Momentum
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading bias on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) despite signs of strengthening bullish momentum.

The research house said the HSIF rose 102 points on Tuesday to close at 26,492 points, supported by improving buying interest as it approached the 26,600-point resistance level.

Intraday, the index opened at 26,393 points, slipped to a low of 26,348 points before rebounding to a high of 26,544 points. However, in evening trade, the index declined 271 points to last trade at 26,221 points.

RHB Research noted that the second consecutive positive price action, alongside a rising relative strength index, indicates that buyers are gaining momentum. A continuation of the uptrend could see the index break above 26,600 points and move towards 27,200 points.

Nonetheless, it cautioned that the broader bearish setup remains intact, suggesting that resistance levels could be strong and profit-taking may emerge.

As such, the research house advised traders to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risks.

Immediate support is seen at 25,100 points, followed by 24,500 points, while resistance levels are pegged at 26,600 points and 27,200 points.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Middle East Tensions Weigh On Sentiment
data.description:
Singapore shares opened weaker, tracking a negative Wall Street session as renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East overshadowed upbeat corporate earnings and resilient US economic data.

The Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 7.43 points or 0.15% to 5,007.53 as at 9.07am, reflecting cautious risk sentiment across regional markets.

Broader market breadth was slightly mixed at the open, with 104 gainers versus 90 decliners, while total volume stood at 147.12 million shares worth S$162.17 million.

Sentiment was weighed by US equities, which closed lower overnight after early gains faded as concerns over the US-Iran conflict resurfaced and overshadowed optimism from corporate earnings and artificial intelligence-driven growth.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.59%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both declined 0.63% and 0.59% respectively.

Markets had initially been supported by strong earnings momentum and expectations of solid economic growth, but sentiment reversed in late trading as geopolitical uncertainty intensified.

In the derivatives market, SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures for April 2026 traded at 3,136, while Nikkei 225 Index Futures for June 2026 stood at 59,550. MSCI Singapore Index Futures were at 449.25.

Global risk sentiment was also shaped by mixed US economic signals, including stronger-than-expected retail sales data, which rose 1.7% in March, the largest increase in a year, driven by higher fuel prices and service station spending.

Despite the broader weakness in equities, energy stocks in the US were the only major sector to post gains overnight, supported by rising oil prices amid geopolitical concerns.

Market participants also monitored corporate developments, including a sharp gain in UnitedHealth Group after it raised its full-year profit outlook, while Apple declined following leadership transition news involving its chief executive succession plan.

data.title: Dollar At Week High As Markets Raise Doubts Over Iran Ceasefire
data.description:
The dollar steadied in early Asian trade on Wednesday, as skepticism over U.S. President Donald Trump’s announcement of an indefinite extension of a ceasefire with Iran lifted demand for the safe-haven currency, pushing it to a one-week high.

On the policy front, Federal Reserve nominee Kevin Warsh’s comments at a Senate confirmation hearing were interpreted as slightly hawkish, while strong retail sales data provided an upbeat view on the strength of the American economy.

The U.S. dollar index, which measures the greenback’s strength against a basket of six currencies, was steady at 98.415, its highest level since April 13. 

“There was a modest risk-off tone overnight amid renewed uncertainty around U.S.–Iran peace talks,” analysts from Westpac wrote in a research report.

Most other currencies were unchanged after the ceasefire extension. The euro was steady at $1.1739 and was up 0.1 per cent at $1.3519. The Australian dollar was little changed at $0.7152 while the New Zealand dollar held steady at $0.5894.

Against the yen, the U.S. dollar nudged down 0.1 per cent to 159.26 yen as data earlier showed Japan’s exports rose for a seventh straight month, defying any major impact from disruptions caused by the Gulf conflict.

The market is troubled by divisions between the hardline Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and more moderate factions within the Iranian government, said Tony Sycamore, market analyst at IG in Sydney. 

“Make no mistake, this internal power struggle remains the single biggest obstacle to any lasting deal, and how it resolves remains to be seen,” he said.

Separately, Trump said on Tuesday he will “remember” companies that do not seek refunds for payments they made on his tariffs that were deemed illegal by the Supreme Court, although he did not specify how companies might benefit by abstaining from the U.S. government’s new refund portal.

U.S. retail sales increased 1.7 per cent in March, more than the expected 1.4 per cent rise as the war with Iran boosted gasoline prices and led to a record surge in receipts at service stations, while tax refunds underpinned spending elsewhere.

REGIME CHANGE AT THE FED

In Washington, Warsh said on Tuesday he had made no promises to Trump about cutting interest rates, as he tried to assure U.S. senators mulling his confirmation to lead the U.S. central bank that he would act independently of the White House while pursuing broad reforms. 

“The most interesting points were probably that he emphasized the Fed’s independence and clearly rejected any request from President Trump to cut rates; taken together, the overall tone could be described as slightly hawkish,” said Junya Tanase, chief Japan FX strategist at JPMorgan Chase & Co in Tokyo.

“That said, since OIS pricing barely moved even after Warsh’s remarks, yesterday’s rise in U.S. yields and the USD’s strength were likely driven mainly by higher oil prices on Iran-related news, suggesting Warsh’s impact was limited.”

Republican Senator Thom Tillis used his time during the hearing to detail why he would delay the confirmation until the Trump administration drops an ongoing criminal probe of current Fed Chair Jerome Powell. 

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin was up 0.2 per cent at $75,894.67, while ether nudged up by the same magnitude to $2,321.92.

Reuters

data.title: Bitcoin Powers Ahead While Ethereum Tracks Gains In Crypto Rally
data.description:
Bitcoin surged into April 22 with strong momentum, holding above the US$76,000 level after a fresh leg-up driven by institutional buying and improving macro sentiment, while Ethereum followed with more modest gains as investors rotated back into risk assets.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency climbed roughly 1%-2% over the past 24 hours to trade near US$76,800, extending a broader rebound from February lows and pushing toward recent highs just below US$78,000.

Ethereum, the second-largest token, meanwhile, lagged slightly but remained firmly in positive territory, rising about 0.7%-1.4% in tandem with Bitcoin’s advance.

The rally was underpinned by a powerful mix of institutional accumulation and macro tailwinds. A major US-listed firm, formerly known as MicroStrategy, unveiled a US$2.5 billion Bitcoin purchase, its largest since 2024, reinforcing corporate demand and boosting market confidence.

At the same time, easing geopolitical tensions and expectations of diplomatic progress in the Middle East lifted broader risk appetite, spilling over into crypto markets alongside gains in US equity futures.

The upswing has also spilled into crypto-linked equities, with firms tied to digital assets, particularly those with direct Bitcoin exposure, outperforming as leverage to the rally amplifies gains.

With Bitcoin flirting with multi-month highs and Ethereum stabilising, analysts are watching for a decisive breakout above the US$78,000 level for Bitcoin, which could open the path toward US$80,000 and beyond.

For now, the latest rally marks a continuation of crypto’s renewed bullish cycle, driven less by speculation and more by institutional capital, macro alignment and strengthening technicals, a combination that could define the next leg of the market.
